description: Add `hal factory run <source> --sandbox` as a sandbox-backed factory executor path that runs the existing `hal auto` pipeline remotely while preserving local factory run records, timelines, metadata, and failure handoff information.

userStories.title: Extend Factory Run Records With Optional Sandbox Metadata
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want sandbox execution metadata stored in local factory run records so that I can inspect which remote environment was used after a sandbox-backed run.
acceptanceCriteria: Factory run records persist an optional sandbox metadata object without requiring sandbox fields for local runs
Sandbox metadata includes sandbox name, provider, lifecycle status, safe connection display fields, and cleanup or handoff instructions
Sandbox metadata omits tokens, private keys, secret environment values, raw credentials, and unsafe environment values
Existing local factory run records remain readable when sandbox metadata is absent
Serialization tests assert JSON field names for the new sandbox metadata object
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document Sandbox Metadata Contract Changes
userStories.description: As an automation maintainer, I want durable factory output contract documentation updated for sandbox metadata so that downstream tools can rely on stable machine-readable fields.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/contracts/factory-run-v1.md` and `docs/contracts/factory-status-v1.md` describe the optional sandbox metadata fields when factory durable output changes
Contract examples include sandbox metadata only for sandbox-backed runs
Contract or documentation sync tests fail if documented sandbox metadata fields drift from implementation JSON fields
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Introduce Injectable Sandbox Executor Dependencies
userStories.description: As a Hal maintainer, I want the sandbox factory executor to accept injectable dependency interfaces for sandbox resolution, provisioning, provider execution, clocks, and run-record persistence so that later sandbox behavior stories can be tested without external side effects.
acceptanceCriteria: A sandbox factory executor dependency struct or interface layer exists for sandbox resolution, sandbox provisioning or start operations, provider execution, clock access, and factory run-record persistence
Production dependencies delegate to existing factory and sandbox helpers without changing local factory execution behavior
Unit tests can instantiate the sandbox executor with fake dependencies for each side-effect boundary
Tests do not require real sandbox providers, Codex, GitHub authentication, network access, or long-running `hal auto`
The sandbox executor dependency layer includes a normalization helper that fills nil dependencies with production defaults, matching the existing `factoryRunDeps` defaulting pattern
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Sandbox Flag To Factory Run Command
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want `hal factory run <source> --sandbox` to explicitly select remote sandbox execution so that local factory execution remains the default.
acceptanceCriteria: `hal factory run` exposes a `--sandbox` flag in Cobra command metadata
`hal factory run <source>` without `--sandbox` invokes the existing local execution path
`hal factory run <source> --sandbox` selects a sandbox-backed execution path
Command help text and examples mention sandbox execution
Tests cover the no-flag local regression path and verify existing local execution dependencies are invoked
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Resolve Existing Sandbox For Factory Execution
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want sandbox-backed factory runs to reuse an existing managed sandbox when one can be resolved so that repeated runs do not unnecessarily create new environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Sandbox-backed factory execution resolves a target sandbox through existing sandbox registry and provider abstractions
Resolution uses `sandbox.ResolveDefault` with the running-only filter when no explicit sandbox target is supplied, preserving the existing empty-state and ambiguity error strings
Resolved sandbox name and provider are recorded in the factory run record
Tests use fake sandbox registry and provider dependencies
Tests cover successful existing sandbox resolution without real provider or network calls
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Provision Or Start Sandbox When None Is Usable
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want sandbox-backed factory execution to provision or start a sandbox when no suitable target is available so that the remote pipeline has an execution environment.
acceptanceCriteria: Sandbox-backed factory execution provisions or starts a sandbox through existing provider abstractions when resolution does not return a usable target
Provisioned sandbox metadata is persisted in the factory run record
Provision or start failures are classified as factory run failures
Failure records include sandbox handoff metadata when a sandbox name or provider is available
Tests cover provision success and provision failure using fake dependencies
Tests perform no real provider calls, network calls, GitHub side effects, or long-running `hal auto` execution
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Execute Remote Hal Auto Through Sandbox Provider
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want sandbox-backed factory runs to invoke the existing `hal auto` pipeline through the sandbox provider execution path so that factory work runs remotely without duplicating pipeline logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Sandbox-backed execution invokes the existing provider `Exec` or SSH-based execution path
Remote command runs the existing `hal auto` pipeline with the source and options required by factory run semantics
Remote command construction is deterministic and covered by tests with injected dependencies
Remote execution receives only redaction-safe environment and configuration values required for the pipeline
Tests assert the exact remote command or structured command arguments passed to the fake provider
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Stream Remote Output Into Factory Timeline
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want useful remote pipeline output to appear in the local factory timeline so that I can monitor sandbox execution from local run records.
acceptanceCriteria: Remote stdout and stderr lines from provider execution are captured by the sandbox executor
Captured remote progress and log lines are recorded as factory timeline events
Timeline events identify the output source as remote sandbox execution
Timeline event payloads are sanitized before persistence
Tests verify remote output lines are converted into sanitized timeline events
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist Successful Sandbox Run Outcome
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want successful sandbox-backed factory runs to leave a complete local record so that run history remains useful even though execution happened remotely.
acceptanceCriteria: On remote execution success, the factory run is marked successful using existing factory run result conventions
Final run record includes sandbox name, provider, final known status, safe connection metadata, and cleanup or handoff instructions
Timeline events include remote execution start, remote pipeline output, and remote execution completion
Existing local factory run success behavior remains unchanged
Tests verify persisted metadata and timeline events for a successful sandbox run
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist Sandbox Failure Handoff Metadata
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want failed sandbox-backed runs to preserve enough information to continue diagnosis so that I can inspect the remote environment manually.
acceptanceCriteria: On remote execution failure, the local factory run record includes sandbox name when known
Failure output includes `hal sandbox ssh <name>` when a sandbox name is available
Saved sandbox metadata includes provider, final known status, safe connection display fields, and diagnostic handoff instructions
Failure timeline events include sanitized remote error output or a sanitized failure summary
Tests cover remote execution failure and verify saved handoff metadata
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update Factory Sandbox CLI Documentation
userStories.description: As a Hal maintainer, I want CLI documentation to describe sandbox-backed factory execution so that users can discover and run the new mode correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI help text documents `hal factory run <source> --sandbox`
Generated CLI documentation is updated when command metadata changes
Command metadata tests assert required sandbox help text and example lines
Documentation generation or docs-check tests pass when generated docs are in sync
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
